reachability_artifacts: built sworn board --json aggregate and named-release invocations demonstrate board-oracle-v1 compatibility and fail-closed malformed projection rejection

effort_complexity.rationale: A new public protocol boundary must preserve compatibility while enforcing cross-node topology and ref-safety semantics.

release_benefit: Lets every later release-mode adapter consume one portable, safe board projection rather than Sworn-private convenience metadata.

design_decisions.choice: Make the Baton board-oracle-v1 projection a shared validated authority with only portable release/track/slice/dependency fields, and make mutable use require an exact verified sourceRef while Git derives optional convenience state.
options: portable aggregate plus named compatibility projection with a reusable validator and guarded sourceRef
retain separate CLI and TUI-local discovery fallbacks
serialize worktree paths, convenience state, or unverified revision expressions as mutable authority
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified by Brad (Coach), 2026-07-18: carry the Baton v0.16.0 board-oracle release delta into this replan as a distinct, fail-closed protocol slice after exact vendor parity.
design_decisions.rationale: One portable validation boundary lets loop, merge, ship, and replan consumers agree on ownership while preventing optional local metadata or an unsafe ref spelling from selecting mutable Git state.

positive_scenarios: Aggregate and named board output conform to portable v0.16 shapes and release-mode consumers can validate a safe selected topology.
negative_scenarios: Duplicate ownership, row mismatch, dependency inconsistency, unsafe sourceRef, and filesystem-only mutation attempts fail before Git path derivation or mutation.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: A validated public projection makes release automation portable across engines without weakening fail-closed topology ownership.
